bibliographic_data.title: Deep Distribution Regression
bibliographic_data.abstract: Due to their flexibility and predictive performance, machine-learning based regression methods have become an important tool for predictive modeling and forecasting. However, most methods focus on estimating the conditional mean or specific quantiles of the target quantity and do not provide the full conditional distribution, which contains uncertainty information that might be crucial for decision making. In this article, we provide a general solution by transforming a conditional distribution estimation problem into a constrained multi-class classification problem, in which tools such as deep neural networks are used. We propose a novel joint binary cross-entropy loss function to accomplish this goal. We demonstrate its performance in various simulation studies comparing to state-of-the-art competing methods. Additionally, our method shows improved accuracy in a probabilistic solar energy forecasting problem.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper proposes a novel joint binary cross-entropy loss function to transform conditional distribution estimation into a constrained multi-class classification problem, enabling the use of deep neural networks for estimating full conditional distributions.
core_content.key_methodology: A two-stage method is proposed: first, partitioning the response space into bins; second, estimating probabilities that the target variable falls into each bin given input covariates. A joint binary cross-entropy loss function is used to couple with a neural network model, ensuring monotonicity of the estimated cumulative distribution function.
outcomes: Superior estimation accuracy in probabilistic solar energy forecasting compared to competing methods
A general solution for transforming conditional distribution estimation into multi-class classification
core_content.full_text_markdown: In recent years, machine learning methods such as random forest and neural networks have gained popularity due to their flexibility. However, most regression methods only provide point estimates without uncertainty information, which is crucial in fields like financial markets and energy forecasting. This paper proposes a method that transforms conditional distribution estimation into a multi-class classification problem using deep neural networks. The authors use a joint binary cross-entropy loss function to ensure the monotonicity of the estimated cumulative distribution function. They demonstrate the effectiveness of their approach through simulation studies and real-world applications, particularly in solar energy forecasting.
